summary: Tested the 2 new changes: (1) Email ID field added to hiring enquiry form, (2) redesigned Hero section desktop layout (2-col grid, form fills right column). Both verified working correctly on backend and frontend across desktop (1920px, 1366px), tablet (768px) and mobile (390px). Backend /api/leads/notify tested directly with pytest - valid submission returns 200 with real Resend email_id, missing required fields correctly return 422. Frontend: email validation (empty + invalid format) shows correct inline errors, full valid submission succeeds with real network call (200), success message and toast both display correctly. Regression pass confirmed: section order (Hero->Recent Closures->Problem), no Metrics section, FAQ present, floating WhatsApp button present, light theme intact.

minor.issue: LeadEnquiry.email is typed as plain 'str' (not pydantic EmailStr), so backend performs no server-side email format validation. When an invalid email like 'notanemail' is sent directly to the API (bypassing frontend zod validation), Resend rejects the malformed reply_to and the endpoint throws an unhandled exception returning HTTP 500 instead of a clean 400/422. Low risk since real users go through the frontend form which validates first, but API is not robust against direct/malicious calls.

action_items: Optional/low priority: change LeadEnquiry.email field to pydantic EmailStr (requires email-validator package) or wrap the Resend call in a try/except that returns 400 for invalid email format instead of leaking a 500 error.
critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx new layout (grid lg:grid-cols-2 lg:items-start) correctly places enquiry form card in right column filling available height, with compact HeroVisual moved under left column copy (hidden below lg breakpoint) - matches requested redesign exactly, no empty white space observed at 1920px or 1366px.

EnquiryForm.jsx email field validation via zod z.string().trim().email() works correctly for both empty and malformed inputs, and real axios call to /api/leads/notify succeeds with toast + success state.
Backend LeadEnquiry model should ideally use EmailStr type for defense-in-depth validation (see minor issue above), but not blocking given low-volume, frontend-validated flow described by user.

success_rate.backend: 100% (5/5 tests passed, 1 documents a known low-priority gap)

test_credentials: None required - no auth for this feature. Resend API key and recipient already configured in backend/.env per /app/memory/test_credentials.md
seed_data_creation: None - no DB persistence by design
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both requested changes (email field + hero layout redesign) fully verified working on backend and frontend across all viewports. Only outstanding item is a low-priority backend robustness gap (500 instead of 422 for malformed email sent directly to API, bypassing frontend validation) - optional fix, not blocking. No retest required unless this or other features change.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no blocking bugs found. Minor gap: LeadEnquiry.email: str (no EmailStr validation) causes Resend API to throw when reply_to is malformed, resulting in unhandled 500 instead of clean 4xx when the endpoint is called directly with an invalid email (frontend already prevents this via zod validation in normal usage).
